findings: Independent live verification confirms Acadian Flycatcher (Empidonax virescens), family Tyrannidae. The candidate's length, wingspan, and weight match Audubon's published ranges exactly; Cornell gives the same length and substantially matching weight, but a narrower wingspan range.

All visible factual claims are supported: upright mid-level perch-and-sally foraging, insects/spiders/small fruits, mature wet deciduous woodland and tropical winter forest, olive upperparts, pale underparts, thin pale eye-ring, two pale wingbars, long wing tips, broad dark-above/orange-yellow-below bill, and square-tipped tail.

The illustration agrees with the supplied reference photos and verified field marks: single peaked-looking head, single broad bicolored bill, pale eye-ring around a dark eye, olive back, pale underparts, dark folded wing with two pale wingbars, long tail, and dark legs/feet. It visibly depicts one head, one beak, two wings, two legs, and one tail with no anatomical defects.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears on the plate.

verification_sources.title: Acadian Flycatcher Identification — All About Birds
